commentaries.author: Cornelius a Lapide
commentaries.content: And another came, saying, Lord, behold, here is thy pound "I give it back to thee, whole, but without gain or increase." "To tie up money in a napkin," says Bede, "is to hide our gifts under the idleness of a lifeless torpor."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: He had Himself fore-shown, by means of a parable, that they should not keep back in secret, fruitless of interest,

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
